items.title: Pinocchio: Unstrung
items.overview: Influenced by a sinister Cricket and created by Geppetto, Pinocchio launches a violent crusade to carve himself into a real boy like his brother James, one piece at a time.

items.title: Ip Man: Kung Fu Legend
items.overview: In 1950s Hong Kong, British merchants and Chinese triads have joined forces to expand their territory, forcibly demolishing factories without regard for public welfare. This move draws fierce opposition from Ip Man, standing up for the workers, he faces pressure from all sides. Framed for murder, he is sent to prison. Within the prison walls, the triads set multiple deadly traps. Can Ip Man overcome these trials, escape the danger, and clear his name?

items.title: Elize: Shadows of a Woman
items.overview: In this retelling of a real case, a former escort discovers her wealthy husband's infidelity and struggles to cope as betrayal poisons their marriage.

items.title: The Dink
items.overview: Desperate to save a struggling country club and earn his father's respect, a washed-up tennis pro is compelled to break a sacred vow and do the unthinkable: play pickleball.

items.title: Euphoria: A Look Back
items.overview: The cast and crew of HBO's Euphoria take an exclusive look back at the critically acclaimed series' origins, evolution, and influences.

items.title: The Truthers
items.overview: Ruth, a 30-year-old woman who has been estranged from her family for some time, returns home when her mother dies under strange circumstances. There, she is reunited with her father, Martín, who since retiring seems to have become a grumpy, aggressive man obsessed with a sinister conspiracy theory. The relationship between the two of them is so tense, and his behavior so erratic, that Ruth begins to suspect Martín might be responsible for her mother's death. Is it possible she's sharing a roof with a murderer?

items.title: Sudesha
items.overview: The story of a woman living in a village in the lower Himalayas. In this area, people depend entirely on the forest for their daily needs of firewood, food and water. But the forests have been destroyed by powerful timber traders. And along with the forest, the livelihood of the people has been greatly altered. The women of this region played an active role in the "Chipkow Movement". Sudesha was imprisoned for her protest.

items.title: Wham! 10 Days in China
items.overview: In 1985, Wham! were the first Western pop group to perform in communist China. George Michael and Andrew Ridgeley’s two concerts in Beijing and Guangzhou introduced live Western pop to Chinese audiences and marked a turning point for the band. For Wham!, the tour was more than music; it was a bold move to capture American attention and launch them into global superstardom, and it worked.

items.title: Jana Nayagan
items.overview: A clash of ideologies. One stands for the people, the other feeds on control. Their paths collided once before. Years later, a child’s silent fear ignites the past, drawing a former police officer into a battle far bigger than personal revenge.

items.title: Spider-Man: Brand New Day
items.overview: Fighting crime full-time as Spider-Man in a world that doesn't remember him—and the pressure of seeing his old friends move on without him—sparks a change in Peter Parker he may not have the power to control. But that transformation might also be the only thing that can stop a shocking new threat to the city and those he loves - a powerful villain no one can even see.

items.title: Soulm8te
items.overview: After a ruthless tech giant acquires his company, a grieving engineer is tasked with testing their new AI companion. But when he attempts to program her to be a truly sentient soulmate, she develops needs of her own—unleashing a relentless spree of precision-engineered mayhem.

items.title: Thingy
items.overview: A young woman will do anything to avoid her ex at a secluded house party. But a supernatural virus passing through the partygoers may take care of him for her.

items.title: Fruit Gathering
items.overview: In industrial Yangon, young factory worker San Kyi dreams of returning to her native village. Her relationship with co-worker Theint Theint Oo evolves from closeness to growing distance, revealing tensions between their expectations and life prospects. Amid exhausting labour, social repression, and economic uncertainty, both women seek escape and intimacy.

items.title: I, Object
items.overview: Grieving the loss of his father and unable to relate to the people in his life, 10-year-old Tom takes refuge in the everyday objects around him; they reveal their true identities and speak to Tom, helping him in his healing process.

items.title: The Dink
items.overview: Desperate to save a struggling country club and earn his father's respect, a washed-up tennis pro is compelled to break a sacred vow and do the unthinkable: play pickleball.

items.title: Anthony Joshua vs. Kristian Prenga
items.overview: Anthony Joshua makes his highly anticipated return to the ring against knockout artist Kristian Prenga in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia.

items.title: Mary Beth Barone: Galaxy Brain
items.overview: This marks Mary Beth Barone's first hour-long comedy special.

items.title: The Devil's Mouth
items.overview: A group of college friends' Thailand adventure turns deadly when they become trapped in submerged caves with a dangerous predator. As oxygen runs low, past conflicts emerge in their desperate fight for survival.
